text: "The powers of magic are reserved for those with the potential to use them.  I sense no such potential in you."
speaker: zara

text: "May this aid you on your Quest!"

say: I detect that you have not the money to purchase that item.  Do not toy with me!

text: Tall shelves with books and odds and ends suited to magic line the walls.  In front of you is a counter with a pentagram embossed on the front.  The ceiling and the far wall are mysterious indeed.  There is a strange symbol on the floor.
speaker: narrator

text: You see a wide variety of arcane objects.  For example:
speaker: narrator

text: A replacement part for a magical face-lift.
speaker: narrator

text: Books dealing with sorcery, necromancy and other occult subjects.
speaker: narrator

text: Bottles of mephitic potions.
speaker: narrator

text: Tinsel and chaff, very good for avoiding radar.
speaker: narrator

text: A hooded robe for when it rains in here.
speaker: narrator

text: Crystal balls, useful only to those initiated into the ways of witches.
speaker: narrator

text: A toaster oven, very rarely used (electricity not invented yet).
speaker: narrator

text: A frog.
speaker: narrator

text: Dried up Mantray (Manta jerky).
speaker: narrator

text: The ceiling is shrouded with clouds of smoke.
speaker: narrator

text: The floor is almost completely covered by a strange symbol.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't see any monsters here.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't see any living creatures.
speaker: narrator

text: This living creature must be some sort of familiar.  There is an uncanny look in its eyes, and you don't want to look at it too closely.  It definitely has magic.
speaker: narrator

text: The walls are covered with shelves full of interesting and very unusual objects.  The back wall behind the counter seems to be not a wall at all, but perhaps an extension of some unearthly place.
speaker: narrator

text: The titles are totally unfamiliar to you.
speaker: narrator

text: Aside from the pentagram on the front, there is nothing unusual about the counter.
speaker: narrator

text: Someone is watching you.
speaker: narrator

text: There is no one here to sell you anything.
speaker: narrator

text: Zara has a mysterious, unearthly look to her.  You have the feeling that she is not a person to trifle with.
speaker: narrator

text: "My name is Zara.  This is my shop."
speaker: zara

text: "I am both human and Faery Folk.  I draw my Power from both."
speaker: zara

text: "We are a people of Power and Magic, and live in the forests far beyond the mountains to the west."
speaker: zara

text: "Monsters stay out of town, and I stay in.  They are of no concern to me."
speaker: zara

text: "Damiano is my familiar and my companion.  We share our lives and our magic."
speaker: zara

text: "When you have mastered nine spells, have the power to cast them all, and proved yourself worthy by accomplishing a great deed, then you must undergo initiation to become a full Wizard."
speaker: zara

text: "There is a place in the distant south where you must journey, but first you must become a hero here."
speaker: zara

text: "A Wizard is one who both shapes Magic and is shaped by it.  Erasmus and I are the only Wizards in this valley."
speaker: zara

text: "There is much Magic in this world for those who know how to use it.  There is Magic in this little town, and a good deal of Magic in this valley."
speaker: zara

text: "What I sell here are merely tools to help those who have power.  You may purchase restorative potions and scrolls from which you may learn spells if you have the potential."
speaker: zara

text: "There is an Aura protecting this town from danger.  Within most of the town's walls, there can be no act of violence or cruel Magic.  Even so, it is prudent to avoid dark places."
speaker: zara

text: "An Aura is a Spell of protection surrounding something.  The town is still protected by the Aura cast by the great Spellcaster, Erana."
speaker: zara

text: "There is much Power in this valley, and it attracts those who use Magic.  I am here in Spielburg, Erasmus has his house on Zauberberg, and even Baba Yaga has her hut cooped up somewhere around here."
speaker: zara

text: "She is a powerful and wicked hag.  You would be wise to avoid her.  She cursed the Baron von Spielburg when he tried to drive her away."
speaker: zara

text: "This is Baba Yaga's curse, cast upon the Baron years ago:"
speaker: zara

text: "Upon von Spielburg and all his clan, this Curse I now demand:  What I will shall come full measure, so shall ye lose all that ye treasure."
speaker: zara

text: "There is always a way to break a curse; possibly Erasmus knows more about this one."
speaker: zara

text: "Baba Yaga's hut is Magical.  It stands on chicken legs, and you must know the rhyme to enter."
speaker: zara

text: "I have no interest in entering Baba Yaga's hut and therefore do not know the rhyme."
speaker: zara

text: "He was once the great leader of this land until he was foolish enough to anger Baba Yaga."
speaker: zara

text: "Erana was a powerful Spellcaster who lived long ago.  She brought peace to this valley.  Even now her spell protects this town from violence or foul Magic."
speaker: zara

text: "Her final resting place is due north of town, and it is a place of both safety and healing.  It is known as `Erana's Peace'"
speaker: zara

text: "Erasmus is a Wizard and a Spellcaster who knows much about this area.  He lives in a tower eastward of town.  He can be very helpful if approached properly, but he has a strange sense of humor."
speaker: zara

text: "It is a steep mountain to the northeast."
speaker: zara

text: "That is the Spell that lets you cast a Magical Flame upon your enemy.  The Spell Scroll will cost you 60 silver."
speaker: zara

text: "That is the Spell that lets you place magical energy upon a weapon.  The energy will be released as magical damage when you hit your enemy."
speaker: zara

text: "It is a Spell that allows you at first to unfasten simple locks and knots.  As you gain in skill, then you may open doors with it.  This Spell Scroll will cost you 30 silver."
speaker: zara

text: "The Fetch Spell allows you to lift or lower a lightweight object from a distance.  The Spell Scroll will cost you 40 silver."
speaker: zara

text: "We sell several Magical Spells on study scrolls.  You may purchase Flame Dart for 60 silver, Fetch for 40 silver, or the Open spell for 30 silver."
speaker: zara

text: "The Scrolls we sell are Magical.  You have but to read the Spell, and you will learn it."
speaker: zara

text: "I also know that you can find a wonderful Spell if you learn the secret of Erana's Peace."
speaker: zara

text: "Secrets are made to be kept."
speaker: zara

text: "We can sell you spell learning scrolls and enchanted potions."
speaker: zara

text: "The Healing Potion will cost you 50 silver.  It is made by the Healer outside of town."
speaker: zara

text: "Vigor Potions restore your Stamina after you have exerted yourself.  They cost 25 silver each."
speaker: zara

text: "Power is the essence of magic, that which the wizard shapes and is shaped by."
speaker: zara

text: "The Power Potion restores your magical energy, and costs 75 silver."
speaker: zara

text: "We have Healing, Vigor, and Power Potions."
speaker: zara

text: "Master the Arts Arcane, use those skills to vanquish the evil curse, and you will become a true Hero!"
speaker: zara

text: Just look at the shelves.
speaker: narrator

text: "We are entities of Magic and Power.  Matters such as that are of little importance."
speaker: zara

text: "We choose only to answer questions which interest us.  Do not persist with uninteresting questions."
speaker: zara

text: "We shall not waste our time further."
speaker: zara

text: "You may choose from the Fetch, Open, or Flame Dart Spells."
speaker: zara

text: "You may choose from the Healing, Vigor, or Power Potions."
speaker: zara

text: "I do not sell such things to such as you."
speaker: zara

text: Zara ignores you.  You would do better if you showed some respect by walking up to the counter before talking to her.
speaker: narrator

text: "So, you return to my shop.  Ask of me what you will, but keep to the point."
speaker: zara

text: "I am Zara, and my companion is Damiano."
speaker: zara

text: "The items in this shop are designed for those skilled in the use of magic.  We have very little for ones such as you who have not been initiated, but what we have could prove useful."
speaker: zara

text: This dark, seedy place is less than you expected of a Thieves' Guild.
speaker: narrator

text: The man seated by the table greets you with a scowl;  Crusher is his usual, amiable self;  and you can't quite make out what lurks behind that massive door.
speaker: narrator

text: You feel uneasy about the sinister mood here.
speaker: narrator

text: "I don't have time to talk to unlicensed scum.  Pay your guild license fee, and then we can talk."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "What do you think this is, your home?  Crusher, deal with this deadbeat."
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: The door is locked, and Boris won't let you in.
speaker: narrator

text: Picking locks in the Thieves' Guild is a singularly stupid idea.
speaker: narrator

text: Crusher ignores you.
speaker: narrator

text: "A Thief's license will cost you 25 silvers.  You will need a license to use any of our services."
speaker: boris

text: "Perhaps I could sell you a good lockpick for fifteen silvers, or a wonderful Acme Thief Tool Kit for one hundred silvers."
speaker: boris

text: "I have no time for idle talk.  Why don't you do something constructive like stealing Otto's yo-yo?"
speaker: thief-guildmaster

text: "I can't hear you.  Please come a little closer to the window."
speaker: boris

text: From his expensive clothes to his carefully trimmed mustache, there is an air of sophistication to this villain.
speaker: narrator

text: Crusher is his usual, amiable self (every bit the Goon).
speaker: narrator

text: The opening in the door is covered with a wire grate, but there is space below it where objects can be passed through.  The mysterious-looking man on the other side is Boris, the cashier and fence for the Guild.
speaker: narrator

text: The ladder up to the tavern is very sturdy.  It has to be to support Crusher's weight.
speaker: narrator

text: It looks like an oversized, well-used dartboard.
speaker: narrator

text: There sure are a lot of daggers in the board.  You wonder where the Chief Thief keeps them all.
speaker: narrator

text: The shadowy figure behind the door is Boris, the cashier and fence for the Guild.
speaker: narrator

text: The floor is surprisingly clean, compared to that of the bar upstairs.
speaker: narrator

text: The plaster is chipping in several places.
speaker: narrator

text: You can tell where the bar is by the seep marks of spilled beer.
speaker: narrator

text: The daggers are nicely made, and apparently designed for throwing.  Besides the Chief Thief's seemingly endless collection, there are two matched sets with differently-colored hilts for playing Dag-Nab-It.
speaker: narrator

text: Even thieves get thirsty.
speaker: narrator

text: You're probably wondering if there is any honor among thieves.
speaker: narrator
